en: Usau, are you home?
Yes, come on in.
Muni, are you home?

ab: kadruwa ku Muni, Muakadhaku yakai ku aunga? nau pelaelanga lu mutauwana ku Muni.
ch: B:Muni不在家耶!我是Muakai，有什麼事嗎? 回來我會轉告Muni。
en: Muni is not at home. I am Muakadhaku. What can I do for you? I will let him know when he comes back.
Who are you?

ab: kaingu, Munia ku si Muakai, muleba inai!
ch: B:奶奶，我們是Muni和Muakai，幫我們開門!
en: Grandma, we are Munia and Muakai, please open the door for us.
Who is it?

ab: Cyamaha ku, wakela ku silape ki Legeai.
en: I am Cyamaha, I am here for Legeai.
Grandma, I am here to see you.

ch: A:你好久沒來了，我好想念你。
en: You haven’t been here for a long time. I missed you so much.

ab: Muni, wakela ku ila musuwa kivaevange.
en: Muni, I am here to play with you.

ab: nyama mudana, maulaingana ku mukalava musuwa.
en: Come on in! I have been waiting for you for a long time.

ch: A:這是我種的地瓜，吃吃看!
en: This is the sweet potato I grow. Try it!
The sweet potato is so sweet.

ab: nyama! lredrekeli kaivai madru, kwanea palalava.
ch: A:過來!這是我種的水果，吃看看。
en: Come here! These are the fruits I grow. You should try it.
The fruit tastes sweet.

ch: A:請坐! 這是我種的有機玉米吃看看!
en: Take a seat! You should try some organic corn that I grow.
Wow! The corn tastes so sweet.
Do you want some oranges?

ab: kadruwaka aimakakane ku tyanese, nau kane ku pingku sikathaa.
ch: B:我不能吃橘子，我吃蘋果就好。
en: I can’t eat oranges. I will have some apples instead.
Would you want some cookies?
Sure, I like it very much.

ab: nau kungicu ikai madru sipakane musuwa.
en: Let me peel the fruit for you.

ab: maelanenga, makalra ku esebe ikai madru.
en: Thank you. This fruit is so juicy.

ab: nau alra ku acilai paungulu musuwa.
en: I will bring you some water.
Thank you.

ab: kwanea pangikalra, makalra ku tudamai li.
en: Enjoy yourself. I cooked a lot of dishes.
Oh! It smells good and looks delicious.
Enjoy yourself. There is a lot more.
I’ll make myself at home then.
Enjoy yourself. There are plenty here.

ch: B:我吃了好多，不能再吃了。
en: I ate a lot and couldn’t eat any more.
